exemplar.title: To what extent does Shell attaining its 2050 sustainability target affect its profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does Shell attaining its 2050 sustainability target affect its profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Keeps sustainability clearly in focus throughout, linking net-zero, CSR and SDGs to financial analysis
Regularly ties ratio trends back to Shellâs strategic priorities
Uses current, organization-specific data (revenue, profits, vote percentages) to ground discussion
weaknesses: Analysis sometimes drifts into generic profitability commentary without explicit sustainability framing
Lacks reference to established sustainability frameworks (e.g., Triple Bottom Line)
Shareholder priority discussion isnât consistently linked to Shellâs long-term sustainability strategy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates strong understanding and partially integrated analysis of sustainability (Band 4). Shell is referenced with detailed data and tools are tied to the organizationâhowever, conceptual integration isnât fully seamless in every section, so a 4/5 is warranted.

strengths: Provides three directly relevant supporting items (annual-report extracts, share-price graphs, stakeholder matrix)
Declaration of authenticity and clear linkage of appendices to text
Supporting graphs in appendices are well documented
weaknesses: Lacks breadth (no government reports or consumer studies beyond a single statistic)
Depth is adequate but not extensive
Appendices arenât consistently referenced in-text (e.g., âsee Appendix 1, Figure Aâ)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although three relevant documents are supplied and sufficiently detailed (Band 3), the lack of breadth and in-text referencing limits depth. A score of 2/4 reflects generally relevant documentation that could be more diverse and better integrated.

strengths: Stakeholder power-interest matrix is applied appropriately to prioritize strategic groups
Net Profit Margin and ROI formulas are correctly stated, sourced and aligned to the research question
Tools cover both qualitative and quantitative angles
weaknesses: Selection omits other common frameworks (PEST, SWOT), limiting analytical scope
Tool-selection rationale is described but not critically justified
Some applications remain descriptive rather than decision-focused
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student makes an adequate selection and application of tools (Band 3). Tools are relevant and correctly applied, but the toolkit is limited and lacks explicit justification, so 3/4 is appropriate.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses recent, segment-level financial data, shareholder-vote percentages and market comparisons effectively
Integrates quantitative ratios and qualitative stakeholder insights
Provides critical reflection on data limitations and methodological constraints
Contextual background on Shellâs scale and SDG alignment is well presented
weaknesses: Analysis lacks multi-year trend data and deeper anomaly evaluation
External market-driver discussion (e.g., oil price shocks) is limited
Regulatory stakeholder analysis could be strengthened with specific case citations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis is thorough with sustained integration of quantitative and qualitative evidence (Band 4). To reach the top band, greater triangulation, deeper anomaly evaluation and extended external drivers discussion would be needed. Hence, a 4/5.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the presented evidence
Explicitly answers the research question and summarizes key findings clearly
Draws logical implications for renewables ROI and portfolio balance
weaknesses: Recommendations lack prioritization and methodological detail for proposed consumer research
Further research suggestions could be more targeted based on analytical gaps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions fully address and are consistent with the research question and analysis (Band 3). They are succinct and supported, warranting the maximum 3/3.

strengths: Clear organization with headings, numbered sections and a coherent flow
Well-structured table of contents that aligns with major sections
weaknesses: Some paragraphs are overly dense and need subdivision for readability
Appendix figures lack consistent captions and in-text references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is appropriate and coherent throughout (Band 2). Minor improvements in paragraphing and appendix linking would enhance readability, but 2/2 reflects its overall effectiveness.

strengths: Includes all required presentation elements: title page, declaration, word count, bibliography and appendices
Citations and appendices are correctly formatted overall
weaknesses: Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting and missing access dates
Figure captions lack legends and source citations, affecting clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all IB requirements (Band 2). Some citation-style inconsistencies and missing figure details prevent perfection, but the work fully includes and formats the required elements, justifying 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: Headings, numbered sections, a table of contents, and logical flow from introduction to conclusion demonstrate appropriate, coherent structure throughout.

The appended documents are directly relevant (financial data, share-price trends) and sufficiently detailed for the analyses performed.  They do not, however, offer a wide range of perspectives (e.g., no government reports or consumer studies beyond a single BCG statistic), so depth and breadth are adequate rather than extensive.
The IA includes the title page details, declaration, word count, bibliography, appendices, and correctly formatted citations, satisfying all required presentation elements.

Three clear supporting items are supplied: Shell annual-report extracts, ExxonMobil and Shell share-price graphs, and the stakeholder matrix image.  This fits the 3-5 document bracket and avoids the "only 1-2" penalty.

Each chosen tool is applied to answer the research question: the matrix prioritises stakeholders, and ratio calculations are interpreted in terms of sustainability investments.  Still, some discussion is descriptive (e.g., repeating that fossil fuels are volatile) and does not always drive decision-focused insights, so relevance is clear but application is only moderately effective.

The student selects recent, segment-level financial data, shareholder-vote percentages, and market comparisons, demonstrating a sound evidence base.  Data are correctly used in calculations and tables, surpassing "adequate" but lacking the variety and triangulation expected for the top band.

Analysis links ratio trends to stakeholder expectations and long-term sustainability, showing mostly effective reasoning with integration of quantitative and qualitative insights.  Nonetheless, evaluation of anomalies, external price drivers, or alternative strategies is brief, so the work falls short of the sustained, in-depth evaluation required for Band 5.

The research question is explicitly answered (âhas the potential to positively impact â¦ long-term profitabilityâ) and supported by summary justification, fulfilling the top-band descriptor for this strand.

Stakeholder mapping and two financial ratios constitute an acceptable toolkit for this investigation, covering both qualitative and quantitative angles.  While appropriate, the selection is limited and omits other commonly expected tools (e.g., PEST, SWOT), so it sits in the âadequateâ rather than âeffectiveâ category.

The concluding statementsârenewablesâ growing ROI, fossil-fuel volatility, need for balanced portfolioâare in line with figures presented in the analysis, indicating consistency with the evidence.

Shell is referenced with detailed, current data (revenues, profit figures, shareholder vote percentages, segment margins).  Tools such as the power-interest matrix, NPM and ROI tables, and comparisons with ExxonMobil keep the organisation central.  Nonetheless, a few sections (e.g., consumer premium statistic, generic comments on fossil-fuel volatility) read more broadly about the industry than Shell specifically, so the integration is strong but not completely seamless.

The student keeps the key concept of sustainability clearly in focus from the title page to the conclusion.  The notion is explained (e.g., references to net-zero, CSR, UN SDGs) and repeatedly linked to investment choices and profitability metrics.  However, the analysis is not consistently framed through sustainability theory in every paragraph; several ratio explanations drift into generic profitability commentary.  This indicates a strong but only partially integrated use of the concept, aligning with Band 4 rather than the fully embedded approach expected for Band 5.

annotations.comment: Tool selection is described but lacks explicit justification; explain why these methods best address the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Verify footnote formatting against IB citation guidelines to ensure consistent presentation of source details.
Main findings integrate key financial data and shareholder sentiment effectively, demonstrating solid analytical grounding.
Integrates consumer willingnessâto-pay data effectively, illustrating market segmentation attitude toward sustainability.
Table 1 presents Net Profit Margin data clearly for 2022, supporting comparative analysis across segments.
Discuss the strategic implications of a negative renewable margin in 2022 to deepen evaluative insight.
Reliance on a singleâyear anomaly weakens robustness; include multiâyear trend data to confirm renewables performance.
Explicitly tie these ratio trends back to Shellâs sustainability goals to reinforce the conceptual integration.
Ensure that URLs in the bibliography are not broken across lines and include access dates where required by the chosen style.
The research question is clearly stated and focused, providing a strong foundation for the investigationâs structure.
Clearly identifies sustainability as the key concept, aligning the investigation with core IB themes right from the outset.
Declaration of authenticity is comprehensive and demonstrates academic integrity, clearly linking supporting documents to the commentary.
The table of contents is well organised and provides clear navigation through the sections, supporting reader orientation.
Consider cross-checking the TOC entries against actual headings to ensure consistency in numbering and section titles.
Provides a concise contextual background on Shellâs scale, revenue mix, and SDG alignment, setting strong analytical foundations.
Explicitly reference established sustainability frameworks (e.g., Triple Bottom Line) in the aim paragraph to deepen conceptual integration.
Augment the discussion with external market drivers (e.g., oil price shocks) to strengthen the evaluation of profit volatility.
The powerâinterest matrix is applied appropriately, providing clear stakeholder prioritisation that supports strategic insight.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Include a legend or key for Figure 1âs axes and stakeholder labels to improve immediate reader comprehension.
Analysis of shareholder priorities is focused on short-term gains without linking to Shellâs long-term sustainability strategy.
This paragraph is overly dense and would benefit from subdivision to improve readability and focus.
Refer explicitly to appendix figures in-text (e.g., âsee Appendix 1, Figure Aâ) to guide the reader to supporting data.
Strengthen government stakeholder analysis by citing specific regulatory cases that have impacted Shellâs operations.
The rationale for selecting Net Profit Margin and ROI is well articulated, demonstrating appropriate methodological alignment.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The ROI formula is correctly stated and sourced, enhancing methodological clarity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Verify classification of capital expenditure in Table 2; unusually high ROI may indicate a reclassification issue.
Clarify factors driving the 2023 ROI reversal between segments, referencing market events or strategic shifts.
Critical reflection on data limitations demonstrates strong evaluative skill and awareness of methodological constraints.
Detail specific survey or focus-group methods when proposing consumer attitude research to strengthen feasibility.
Conclusion succinctly addresses the research question and is well supported by the preceding analysis.
Further research recommendations would benefit from prioritizing the most critical next steps based on identified analytical gaps.
Bibliography entries are comprehensive but exhibit inconsistent formatting; align all references to a single citation style.
Appendices provide essential supporting graphs, demonstrating thorough documentation of external data.
Label figure axes and include a source citation in the caption to improve clarity and academic rigour.
Provide consistent captions for each appendix figure (e.g., âAppendix 2: â¦â) and reference them in the main text for coherence.
